data.title: sprint-list-epics.sh fails with 'Argument list too long' when ticket volume is high
data.description:
## Problem

When running `.claude/scripts/dso sprint-list-epics.sh --max-children=0`, the script fails with:

```
/usr/local/bin/python3: Argument list too long
```

This occurs at line 211 of the script when the ticket system has a large number of tickets. The inline Python invocation exceeds the OS argument size limit (ARG_MAX).

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Have a ticket system with a large volume of tickets (observed with ~795KB of JSON output from `ticket list`)
2. Run `.claude/scripts/dso sprint-list-epics.sh --max-children=0`
3. Script exits 126 with 'Argument list too long'

## Expected Behavior

The script should handle large ticket volumes without hitting OS argument size limits. The JSON data should be piped via stdin to python3 rather than passed as a command-line argument.

## Workaround

Use `.claude/scripts/dso ticket list --type=epic` and filter in a separate python3 pipeline via stdin.
